page: Microsoft Azure D16ps_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Dpsv6 server family. It is powered by an ARM64 architecture featuring a dedicated Microsoft Neoverse-N2 processor. This CPU operates at 3.4 GHz with 16 physical cores and 16 vCPUs, meaning there is no hyperthreading. The processor is equipped with 64 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 128 MB of L3 cache. It includes hardware-assisted cryptography via AES support, though it lacks AVX, AMX, and other x86-specific instruction sets.

The instance is configured with 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. It does not include any local storage, relying instead on external Premium Storage capabilities. There are no complimentary public IPv4 addresses bundled with the instance. The underlying virtualization is managed by the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor.

In benchmark testing, the D16ps_v6 demonstrates strong memory performance, achieving a top-tier PassMark memory score and strong uncached read and write memory bandwidths of 300.63 GB/sec and 111.74 GB/sec, respectively. Single-threaded gzip compression is also strong at 33.12 GB/sec. Most other compute benchmarks, including Geekbench, PassMark CPU, and stress-ng, fall within the average range. Large-model LLM inference is a relative bottleneck, with prompt processing on a medium 7B model registering as weak. Given its balanced resource profile and strong memory bandwidth, this server is well-suited for general-purpose workloads, web serving, application hosting, and database operations that leverage ARM-optimized software stacks.
description: Microsoft Azure D16ps_v6 is an ARM64-based general-purpose virtual machine from the Dpsv6 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features a dedicated Microsoft Neoverse-N2 processor operating at 3.4 GHz with 16 physical cores, 16 vCPUs, and a 128 MB L3 cache. The server is equipped with 64.0 GB of RAM, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB per core, but contains no local storage, relying on Premium Storage instead. Benchmark results show top-tier PassMark memory performance and strong uncached memory bandwidth, alongside average CPU performance in Geekbench and stress-ng tests. Lacking x86-specific vector extensions, it relies on ARM-native optimizations. This instance is suitable for general-purpose applications, web serving, and database workloads.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D16ps_v6 is an ARM64 general-purpose virtual machine featuring 16 dedicated Neoverse-N2 cores, 64 GB of RAM, and strong memory bandwidth performance for balanced workloads.

tagline: An ARM64-powered virtual machine with dedicated Neoverse-N2 cores and strong memory bandwidth for balanced general-purpose workloads.
